description_long: This is a heavy-duty hammer with a robust, dark metallic head and a thick, cylindrical wooden handle. The head features a toothed, adjustable jaw mechanism, indicating it is a pipe wrench hammer hybrid, commonly used for gripping and turning pipes in addition to striking. The metal head is dark, likely steel or iron, and shows signs of wear and use, with some surface rust and scratches. The wooden handle is stained and slightly worn, with a smooth, rounded end for comfortable gripping. The tool is utilitarian, designed for durability and function in urban maintenance or construction settings. Its overall appearance suggests it is well-used, reliable, and built to withstand tough environments.
description: A rugged hammer with a dark metal head and a thick, worn wooden handle, featuring an adjustable jaw mechanism. The tool shows signs of heavy use and is built for durability.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular striking face and the adjustable jaw mechanism, with the wooden handle receding into the background.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full length of the wooden handle and the profile of the metal head, clearly showing the toothed jaw and adjustment screw.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the rounded end of the wooden handle, with the head partially obscured and the adjustment mechanism visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the handle and the other side of the metal head, emphasizing the jaw's structure and adjustment features.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, smooth with visible wear and a rounded end.
attribute_head_type: Adjustable pipe wrench head with toothed jaws and a flat striking face, made of dark metal.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible wear on the wooden handle, surface rust, and scratches on the metal head.
